title: Draft metadata interface
description: The contract the PDF-reading agent (Stage 2) fills in from a draft's extract.json. Stage 3 (audit) derives a rename plan from `constructs`, `chapters`, and `extra_renames`; Stage 4 (apply) executes it. RENDERED fields reach the deployed site via apply (chapter num/slug/title/era_*/concept/teaser → front matter + _data/cards.yml; construct ids/slugs/labels/aliases → renames). METADATA-ONLY fields are the source of truth but are NOT propagated to the site: chapter `summary` (written only into NEW chapter stubs), `pitch`, `key_terms`; construct `definition` (the _landing prose is hand-authored). See HARNESS.md and `apply-rename.py --explain-fields`.

ingested.description: ISO date the draft was read

title_line.description: subtitle as printed on the title page

aliases_deprecated.description: old display spellings to rename to canonical_name (case variants auto-generated by the auditor). Emitted as a phrase guard REGARDLESS of status, so a construct kept at status 'unchanged' can still carry these to catch a reintroduced deprecated spelling.

preserve_terms.description: bare words that LOOK related but must NOT be renamed (e.g. 'ecofascism' the movement)

definition.description: canonical definition, verbatim from the draft. METADATA-ONLY: not written to _landing/<slug>.md (the landing prose is hand-authored); apply does not propagate it.

chapters.description: Authoritative chapter list (order + titles from the draft TOC).

prev_slug.description: old site slug this maps from; null if new

era_id.description: scrolly-step id, if this chapter is a timeline step

concept.description: mechanism named in the chapter. RENDERED → _data/cards.yml card 'concept'.

pitch.description: 1–2 sentence pitch for the TOC card. METADATA-ONLY: no live render target (chapters.yml is a phantom).

summary.description: paragraph summary. METADATA-ONLY for existing chapters: written only into NEW chapter stubs by apply.

key_terms.description: METADATA-ONLY: not written to any rendered file.

extra_renames.description: Special-case renames not derivable from constructs/chapters (e.g. a JS component symbol). Use sparingly; everything here is applied verbatim and flagged in the audit report for human review.

definition_blocks.description: Substantive prose rewrites: replace a unique anchor block with new text (used where a rename alone wouldn't capture the new framing).

find.description: unique substring anchor present in the file
